text.title: azure-private-link Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for azure-private-link. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every Private Endpoint MUST have a matching Private DNS zone linked to every VNet that needs to resolve it. The zone name MUST match the service-specific `privatelink.*` zone (see table below). The VNet link MUST have `registration_enabled = false`. Without a valid zone link, DNS from the consumer VNet falls back to the public IP silently.
rule.rationale: Private Endpoint DNS works by overriding the public CNAME chain. Without the Private DNS zone and VNet link, `nslookup storage.blob.core.windows.net` from inside the VNet returns the public IP — the connection works but goes over the internet, defeating the Private Endpoint entirely.
